item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The first image that was used in this article was non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the image comes from here and requires purchase to use. As such, that image has been removed. The other image comes from here and is public domain, making it cc-compliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's nothing to this SCP that feels sarkic, it's just a generic tech-hating cult of evil cavemen with some Sarkicism shoehorned in to make this feel like it fits into a greater mythos. Also, why are the sarkicists and Ion referring to Yaldabaoth as "Our Lord" and worshiping it and trying to aid it? Isn't the entire point of Sarkicism to do the exact opposite?

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You could add "17th century Europe" here to be less ambiguous.

Will do.

Also, what do you mean by "violent mutation"

I'll go into further detail with that.

and why is the race of the D-classes mentioned? It does not appear to be relevant.

It was originally just so that it seemed more or less like the D-Class weren't just numbers on a screen, but I can see how that didn't really affect anything. I'll go back and edit it.

Thank you for your feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am not sure if the usage of the term "civilisation" here, without any further descriptives, is appropriate. When you say "civilization similar to that which was commonplace in the late 17th century", do you mean European civilisation? East Asian civilisation? Middle East? Africa? You could add "17th century Europe" here to be less ambiguous.

Also, what do you mean by "violent mutation", and why is the race of the D-classes mentioned? It does not appear to be relevant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you just haven't seen enough Series IV SCiPs if this is the first you have seen. lmao

Thank you for your comment, and, more importantly, thank you for reading and voting!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dang, first good SCP I found in series 4

The concept is interesting, i think there are a lot of different directions you could go with this.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Found a possible error:

There are established policing forces within SCP-3826, most of them being occupied by the larger instances of SCP-3826-A

I think that last bit is supposed to refer to -1 instead of -A.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed with Sir Baubius. I liked it the first time, and I like it even more now, especially since all my complaints were addressed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I appreciate the kind words and appreciation of my effort! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is way better than the previous rendition. Easy +1.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thank you to Linguafex on #site19 for assisting with vocabulary usage and conceptual modifications.

I hope you enjoyed this second rendition of SCP-3826. Thank you for reading!

Jupiter churns...
– hide block

..was a reference to SCP-2399's usage in the TwistedGears-Kaktus Proposal.
